label: Sample A · 文章 cover (three-oils)
prompt: A horizontal 4:3 editorial still-life photograph of three glass bottles arranged in a quiet ceremonial line on a sheet of raw oatmeal-coloured linen draped across an aged wood tabletop with visible grain and small scuffs of long use. From left to right: a tall amber-glass apothecary bottle holding olive oil whose surface catches a warm green-gold glint, a slightly shorter frosted-glass bottle of pale coconut oil that softly diffuses the light into a milky glow, and a slender clear-glass bottle of palm oil tinged deep amber-red where the daylight passes through it. In the foreground, a small worn wooden stirring spoon rests at a slight diagonal beside a single dried bay leaf, its edges curled. Soft overhead north-facing daylight rakes the scene from upper-left, leaving long gentle shadows across the linen weave. Shallow depth of field, slight medium-format film grain, low-saturation palette of raw linen beige, ink black, muted gold and charcoal. East Asian artisanal soap atelier mood, ceremonial and contemplative. Absolutely no people, no text, no logos, no labels, no modern plastic packaging — only natural materials.

label: Sample B · 海棠 02 packaged (uses 01.png as reference)
prompt: Using the soap shown in the reference image as the exact same bar (preserve its colour, surface texture, and proportions), generate a square 1:1 editorial product photograph of that same hand-pressed rectangular soap bar of approximately 105 grams now fully wrapped in a sheet of unbleached kraft paper folded with two clean creases over the top, tied snugly around the middle with a length of natural raw twine knotted in a simple loose bow, and sealed on the front face with a small irregular dark-red wax stamp the diameter of a coin — the stamp is plain, organic in shape, completely featureless with no letters or insignia. The wrapped bar rests at a 45-degree three-quarter angle on an aged wood tabletop whose surface shows the faint marks of years of use, a small sprig of dried calophyllum (tamanu) leaf placed deliberately just behind it, and a folded raw linen cloth visible at the lower edge. Soft directional north-facing daylight from the upper-left grazes across the kraft paper revealing the fibre texture and the subtle hand-folded creases of the wrap. Shallow depth of field, slight grain, low-saturation warm neutral palette of raw linen beige, ink black, muted gold (#C8A24A) and charcoal. Hand-tied, slow-craft, gift-like atmosphere. No people, no readable text, no logos, no printed labels, no plastic.

label: Sample C · 手繪解說圖 (trace ribbon)
prompt: A hand-drawn 4:3 horizontal illustration in the manner of a Japanese sumi-e brush study crossed with mid-century linocut, executed in a single warm earth tone of muted sepia-gold (#C8A24A) on textured cream paper. The drawing depicts a macro top-down view of a wide round mixing bowl seen from directly above, its rim a confident single line, the cold-process soap batter inside rendered as a smooth pale field crossed with a long flowing ribbon trail where a wooden spoon has been lifted out — the ribbon falls in two or three distinct loose loops across the surface, pooling slightly at one end to suggest viscosity. The wooden spoon angles up and out of the frame in the upper-right, its handle suggested with a few sure strokes. Light cross-hatching in the same earth tone pools along the inner curve of the bowl and beneath the spoon to suggest depth, but the rest of the page remains airy negative space. Crisp ink contour lines, a single restrained wash, the feeling of an old craft-school textbook plate. No photorealism, no colour beyond the single warm tone, no shadows cast outside the bowl, no text, no signature, no logos, no border.

label: Sample D · 成分標籤背景（待覆蓋文字）
prompt: A square 1:1 editorial top-down photograph of a single small handmade paper tag — roughly the size of a playing card, perhaps 60 by 90 millimetres — lying flat and slightly off-centre on an aged wood tabletop. The tag is cut from cream-coloured deckle-edge handmade paper showing a soft fibrous deckle border on all four sides and a faint subtle texture across its surface; the surface is utterly blank, completely unmarked, with absolutely nothing printed, drawn, stamped, or written on it. A narrow ribbon of dark red sealing wax runs horizontally along the very top edge of the tag, slightly thicker than a ribbon, with the organic uneven thickness of poured wax. In the upper-left corner of the tag rests a single small natural dried leaf — perhaps a calophyllum or laurel leaf — its edges curled with age, placed gently as if just set down. Soft directional north-facing daylight from the upper-left brings out the wood grain around the tag and catches the fibres of the paper. Shallow grain, low saturation, warm neutral palette of raw linen beige and ink black and muted gold. The tag's surface must remain a clean empty canvas — no characters, no marks, no decoration, no text of any kind, no calligraphy — only the wax ribbon and the leaf as embellishment. No people, no logos, no plastic.
